raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint regarding Barclays Bank Delawares handling of a credit card dispute under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) and Regulation Z ( 12 CFR 1026.13 ). 

The dispute concerns a charge dated XX/XX/year>, in the amount of {$23.00} from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX on my XXXX XXXX  XXXX credit card ( last four digits : XXXX ). The transaction occurred via contactless payment using XXXX XXXX on the phone of my son, an authorized user on the account. The charge was accidental, occurred without intent to purchase, and no goods or services were received. A similar accidental contactless charge occurred earlier the same day and was refunded by the merchant. When my son later contacted the merchant regarding the {$23.00} charge, the merchant refused to refund it, citing a change in business ownership. 

I disputed the charge with Barclays. Barclays initially issued provisional credit and canceled the card, but later reversed the credit and determined the charge to be valid. When I requested an explanation and supporting evidence, Barclays stated only that the charge method was consistent with prior transactions. No merchant documentation, proof of delivery, proof of performance, or other evidence was provided. Although Barclays briefly stated the investigation was reopened, the disputed amount was not suspended and the original determination was reaffirmed.

I believe Barclays failed to conduct a reasonable investigation as required by Regulation Z 1026.13 ( f ). My dispute was based on the absence of goods or services received, yet Barclays appears not to have obtained or evaluated any merchant evidence addressing delivery or performance. Reliance solely on internal transaction pattern analysis does not satisfy the FCBAs investigation requirements. Additionally, Barclays failed to provide a sufficient written explanation or supporting documentation as required by 1026.13 ( g ).

I am requesting that Barclays be required to correct this billing error, restore the disputed amount, and ensure compliance with the Fair Credit Billing Act and Regulation Z.
